sections.title: Summary
sections.content:
| Layer | Files | Estimated Coverage | Critical Gaps |
|---|---|---|---|
| Frontend unit | ~82 source files | ~55% | Services, PWA, offline handlers |
| Server unit | ~80+ source files | ~10% | All use-cases, repositories, domain logic |
| E2E | 8 flows | ~40% of user journeys | Error paths, offline, edge cases |

---

sections.title: 1. Server — Untested Use-Cases (HIGH PRIORITY)
2. Server — Repository Layer (HIGH PRIORITY)
3. Frontend — Service Layer (MEDIUM PRIORITY)
4. Frontend — Offline/PWA Handler (MEDIUM PRIORITY)
5. E2E — Error Path Gaps (HIGH PRIORITY)
6. Server — JWT / Encryption Edge Cases (HIGH PRIORITY)
Priority Matrix
sections.content:
| Priority | Area | Risk if untested |
|---|---|---|
| **P0** | Server use-cases (register, checkin, stack) | Business logic bugs silent in prod |
| **P0** | JWT signing/verification | Auth bypass if tampered token accepted |
| **P1** | Server repositories | DB writes/reads silently incorrect |
| **P1** | SyncQueue offline flush | Data loss on reconnect |
| **P1** | E2E login error paths | Users stuck with no error feedback |
| **P2** | CheckinService retry logic | Duplicate or lost submissions |
| **P2** | OfflineHandler lifecycle | Memory leaks, stale listeners |
| **P3** | Legal/FAQ pages | Low risk, static content |

The biggest gap is the server-side: controllers have ~10% coverage but the use-cases and repositories underneath them — where most bugs live — have none. That's where I'd focus first.
